text.title: spatial-accessibility Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for spatial-accessibility as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: LLMs defaulting to controller-only assumptions without enumerating alternatives explicitly.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Controllers are unavailable or unusable in many spatial contexts. Force the agent to enumerate all modality alternatives for each interaction rather than assuming a default input path.

failure-mode.description: Agent recommending head-locked HUD as a standard pattern from older XR literature.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Apple HIG visionOS forbids head-locked UI; Meta guidelines strongly discourage it for non-telemetry use. Current guidance is the opposite of legacy XR documentation that LLMs were trained on.

failure-mode.description: Declaring spatial accessibility compliance without hardware testing on real assistive users.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Synthetic XR a11y testing tools are immature. Motion sickness is individually variable and cannot be detected by static analysis. Only real-user hardware testing produces valid compliance evidence.

failure-mode.description: Agent recommending head-locked captions universally without considering immersion context.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Caption placement reasoning is poor in current LLMs. Head-locked captions are readable but break immersion; world-locked captions are immersive but unreadable in motion. Heuristics plus human review are required.
